Most other slices remain too thin or mixed to trust. No material change possible versus prior because this is the first observation window.

warnings: total n=53 still small; many high-WR buckets sit just above the n=8 cutoff

positive EV concentrated in low-spread, dead-volume, neutral-funding regimes only

history.summary: With n=53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG (n=26, wr=0.692), trending regime (n=50, wr=0.64), normal BB state (n=16, wr=0.875), bearish_aligned MTF (n=12, wr=0.833), sell_pressure CVD (n=19, wr=0.737), 120-240s TTC (n=26, wr=0.731), and strong_trend ADX (n=31, wr=0.645) show confirmed positive EV above breakeven with n>=8. UP signals, >=240s TTC, BB expansion/squeeze, and active volume remain marginal or negative despite some positive avg_ev. Sample still small overall; patterns require further confirmation.

With n=53 settled trades, confirmed positive EV patterns (win-rate above breakeven, n>=8) center on DOWN_STRONG signals, trending/Hurst, zscore -2..1, TTC 120-240s, BB normal, volume dead, bearish_aligned MTF, sell_pressure CVD and strong_trend ADX; overall EV ~0.116 but total PnL near flat due to variance in weaker buckets.

With n=53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (esp. zscore -2..1, ttc 120-240s, bb_normal, bearish_aligned mtf, cvd sell_pressure, adx strong_trend) show confirmed edge: win-rates 0.65-0.83, positive EV after costs, and material positive pnl. UP direction and most other buckets remain marginal or negative. Sample still small; many buckets ignored due to n<8.

With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (n=50), bb_normal (n=16), ttc 120-240s (n=26), bearish_aligned mtf (n=12), and sell_pressure cvd (n=19) show confirmed positive EV and win-rates well above breakeven after costs; UP signals and >=240s ttc remain negative. Most other buckets are still too small or mixed to confirm. Sample remains modest so patterns could still shift.

With 53 settled trades, confirmed profitable patterns (n>=8, win-rate >~0.60 with positive EV) center on DOWN direction/strong signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, strong ADX trend, 120-240s TTC, -2..1 z-score, sell-pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and range-middle entries; overall EV remains modestly positive but driven by a few high-performing buckets. Most other slices show either insufficient samples or mixed/negative results consistent with noise. DOWN_STRONG and bearish-aligned conditions stand out as robust while UP and long-TTC buckets are weak.
changes_since_last: first analysis; sample now 53 settled trades with positive aggregate EV

UP signals, >=240s TTC, active volume, and expansion/squeeze BB states remain negative or marginal. Overall edge is modest but directionally consistent after costs.

With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, bearish MTF alignment, sell-pressure CVD, and z-score -2..1 buckets show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.65-0.83, n>=12) after costs. UP signals, long TTC (>=240s), active volume, and expansion/squeeze BB states remain unprofitable or noisy despite some positive avg EV. Overall edge is modest but real in select slices; most other buckets are under-sampled or overfit.

With 53 settled trades the bot shows modest positive EV (overall WR 0.604, avg_ev_after_cost 0.116) driven by a handful of buckets that already exceed n=8 and clear breakeven; DOWN_STRONG, normal BB state, 120-240 s TTC, strong-trend ADX and bearish-aligned MTF stand out as confirmed. Most other slices remain too thin or mixed to trust. No material change possible versus prior because this is the first observation window.
changes_since_last: first analysis window; no prior results to compare

headlines: Satoshi-era dormant whale wallet awakens and sells 13,700 BTC in one go (reported ~06:37 GMT)
BTC ETF outflows continue: ~$1.79B over 5 days, price action near $60k with deleveraging
Recent X discussion on spot selling via ETFs rotating capital to semis/KI, un-cleared longs

note.summary: 61.2% win rate on 103 settled trades yields +$2.71 (profit factor 1.01), but calibration shows the model is NOT predicting direction better than random (signal hit rate 45.3% vs 38.8% baseline is statistically noise). The 83-down/20-up split and 63.9% down-win vs 50% up-win suggests a DOWN structural bias, not skill. Execution gate rejected 168 trades for 'underdog_price_below_floor,' indicating the bot is sniping mispriced underdog tailâthis is the *actual* edge, not directional forecasting.

knob_recommendations.reason: No context slices have â¥50 settled trades; raise threshold to prevent overfitting phantom patterns in small-n buckets
DOWN trades show 63.9% win rate vs 50% UP; tighten DOWN filter to entry_price â¤0.38 to preserve structural underdog-snipe edge
UP trades are 50% win rate (coin flip) with -$6.35 PnL; disable UP until â¥100 samples show >55% win rate
168 rejects for underdog_price_below_floor; current floor is too highâlower to 0.25 to capture more mispriced tail without violating tick size

new_lessons.rule: TradingView MTF signal hit rate 45.3% (vs 38.8% base) is statistically zero; do NOT trade on directional convictionâedge is mispricing arbitrage only
DOWN: 63.9% win, +$7.71 PnL (n=83); UP: 50% win, -$6.35 PnL (n=20). Asymmetry is not alphaâit's market structure (underdog floor rejects 168 UP candidates). Exploit DOWN underdog tail only.
Brier 0.243 < baseline 0.25 but log-loss 0.683 is poor; edge_model calibration bins show no monotonic trend (0.3-0.4 bin: 31.4% empirical_up). Model is curve-fit noiseâdo NOT size by confidence.

168/175 rejects are underdog_price_below_floor; the gate is correctly filtering non-mispriced bets. The 103 accepted trades are *selection artifacts*âloosen floor to 0.25 cautiously and measure if win rate holds.
PF 1.01 on 103 trades is <1 std dev from breakeven (avg_loss $4.63 vs avg_win $2.98 suggests tail risk). Do NOT scale until PF â¥1.15 on â¥200 settled trades.

lessons.rule: TradingView 2-4min timeframe signals have no predictive edge on 5min BTC outcomes (45% vs 39% base); treat as noise, not signal
DOWN win rate (63.4%) is higher than UP (50%), but DOWN trades were 4x more frequentâthis is filter selection bias, not a tradable DOWN edge
168/277 execution gate rejections for underdog_price_below_floor indicate edge model outputs sub-viable edges; tighten model threshold or raise gate floor
Profit factor 1.0007 with avg_loss > avg_win means any transaction cost increase or slippage wipes edge; do not trade until PF > 1.05 on 500+ samples
TradingView MTF signal hit rate 45.3% (vs 38.8% base) is statistically zero; do NOT trade on directional convictionâedge is mispricing arbitrage only
DOWN: 63.9% win, +$7.71 PnL (n=83); UP: 50% win, -$6.35 PnL (n=20). Asymmetry is not alphaâit's market structure (underdog floor rejects 168 UP candidates). Exploit DOWN underdog tail only.
Brier 0.243 < baseline 0.25 but log-loss 0.683 is poor; edge_model calibration bins show no monotonic trend (0.3-0.4 bin: 31.4% empirical_up). Model is curve-fit noiseâdo NOT size by confidence.

168/175 rejects are underdog_price_below_floor; the gate is correctly filtering non-mispriced bets. The 103 accepted trades are *selection artifacts*âloosen floor to 0.25 cautiously and measure if win rate holds.
PF 1.01 on 103 trades is <1 std dev from breakeven (avg_loss $4.63 vs avg_win $2.98 suggests tail risk). Do NOT scale until PF â¥1.15 on â¥200 settled trades.
